blocks.html_en: Introduction

sentences.en: Introduction
sentences.zh: 介绍

blocks.html_en: In aerobic organisms, including humans, CO2 is produced as a by-product of cellular respiration and needs to be removed from the body as a waste product via expiration (Raven et al., 2007). CO2 crosses the cell plasma membrane, enters the blood and almost all of it (90–95%) diffuses into the red blood cells, where it is rapidly hydrated to hydrogen (H+) and bicarbonate (HCO3−) by the enzyme carbonic anhydrase (CA) (Arlot-Bonnemains et al., 1985; Lan et al., 2025). HCO3− in blood is the most important means of transport for CO2 throughout the body (Sherwood, 2013) and when that blood reaches the lungs, the reaction is reversed. HCO3− in the lungs combines with H+ produced by the oxygenation of deoxyhemoglobin to produce water and CO2, which is exhaled as a waste product (Lan et al., 2025). Carbonic anhydrase thus allows a large pool of otherwise slowly reacting plasma HCO3− to be utilized in CO2 excretion (Arlot-Bonnemains et al., 1985). A quantitative relationship exists between plasma HCO3− and levels of CO2, in the blood whereby when CO2 increases, so does HCO3− and vice versa. This is evident in multiple clinical situations (Martinu et al., 2003; Ueda et al., 2009). Importantly, abnormal CO2 retention, or impaired CO2 elimination, which is seen in diseases (Mendez et al., 2019; Palmer & Clegg, 2023), impaired ventilation, or excessive CO2 inhalation (Robertson, 2006), can result in reduced blood pH. The body then attempts to buffer this ‘acidosis’ by various mechanisms, including increased ventilation (if possible), increased renal excretion of acid and conservation of filtered HCO3− (Sherwood, 2013), retention of calcium, phosphates and other substances (Gray et al., 1973) and nervous system stimulation to counteract the direct effects of pH changes on heart contractility and vasodilation (Burton, 1978; Eckenhoff & Longnecker, 1995). Calcium and phosphate (PO₄³⁻) play important supporting roles in maintaining blood acid-base balance, working alongside the HCO3− buffer system. Phosphate can accept or donate hydrogen ions to help stabilize pH, and when blood becomes acidic, calcium and phosphate can be released from bone to help neutralize excess acid (Salcedo-Betancourt & Moe, 2024).

sentences.en: In aerobic organisms, including humans, CO2 is produced as a by-product of cellular respiration and needs to be removed from the body as a waste product via expiration (Raven et al., 2007).
sentences.zh: 在包括人类在内的需氧生物体中，二氧化碳是细胞呼吸的副产品，需要通过呼气作为废物从体内清除（Raven 等，2007）。

sentences.en: CO2 crosses the cell plasma membrane, enters the blood and almost all of it (90–95%) diffuses into the red blood cells, where it is rapidly hydrated to hydrogen (H+) and bicarbonate (HCO3−) by the enzyme carbonic anhydrase (CA) (Arlot-Bonnemains et al., 1985; Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: CO2 穿过细胞质膜，进入血液，几乎全部 (90–95%) 扩散到红细胞中，在红细胞中通过碳酸酐酶 (CA) 快速水合为氢 (H+) 和碳酸氢盐 (HCO3−)（Arlot-Bonnemains 等人，1985 年；Lan 等人，2025 年）。

sentences.en: HCO3− in blood is the most important means of transport for CO2 throughout the body (Sherwood, 2013) and when that blood reaches the lungs, the reaction is reversed.
sentences.zh: 血液中的 HCO3− 是二氧化碳在全身最重要的运输方式（Sherwood，2013），当血液到达肺部时，反应会发生逆转。

sentences.en: HCO3− in the lungs combines with H+ produced by the oxygenation of deoxyhemoglobin to produce water and CO2, which is exhaled as a waste product (Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: 肺部的 HCO3− 与脱氧血红蛋白氧化产生的 H+ 结合，产生水和 CO2，并作为废物呼出（Lan 等，2025）。

sentences.en: Carbonic anhydrase thus allows a large pool of otherwise slowly reacting plasma HCO3− to be utilized in CO2 excretion (Arlot-Bonnemains et al., 1985).
sentences.zh: 因此，碳酸酐酶允许大量反应缓慢的血浆 HCO3− 用于 CO2 排泄（Arlot-Bonnemains 等，1985）。

sentences.en: A quantitative relationship exists between plasma HCO3− and levels of CO2, in the blood whereby when CO2 increases, so does HCO3− and vice versa.
sentences.zh: 血浆 HCO3− 和血液中 CO2 水平之间存在定量关系，当 CO2 增加时，HCO3− 也会增加，反之亦然。

sentences.en: This is evident in multiple clinical situations (Martinu et al., 2003; Ueda et al., 2009).
sentences.zh: 这在多种临床情况中都很明显（Martinu 等人，2003 年；Ueda 等人，2009 年）。

sentences.en: Importantly, abnormal CO2 retention, or impaired CO2 elimination, which is seen in diseases (Mendez et al., 2019; Palmer & Clegg, 2023), impaired ventilation, or excessive CO2 inhalation (Robertson, 2006), can result in reduced blood pH.
sentences.zh: 重要的是，异常的二氧化碳潴留或二氧化碳消除受损（Mendez 等，2019；Palmer & Clegg，2023）、通气受损或过量二氧化碳吸入（Robertson，2006）可能导致血液 pH 值降低。

sentences.en: The body then attempts to buffer this ‘acidosis’ by various mechanisms, including increased ventilation (if possible), increased renal excretion of acid and conservation of filtered HCO3− (Sherwood, 2013), retention of calcium, phosphates and other substances (Gray et al., 1973) and nervous system stimulation to counteract the direct effects of pH changes on heart contractility and vasodilation (Burton, 1978; Eckenhoff & Longnecker, 1995).
sentences.zh: 然后，身体尝试通过各种机制来缓冲这种“酸中毒”，包括增加通气（如果可能）、增加肾脏排泄酸和保存过滤后的 HCO3−（Sherwood，2013）、保留钙、磷酸盐和其他物质（Gray 等，1973）以及刺激神经系统以抵消 pH 值变化对心脏收缩性和血管舒张的直接影响（Burton，1978；Eckenhoff 和 Longnecker， 1995）。

sentences.en: Calcium and phosphate (PO₄³⁻) play important supporting roles in maintaining blood acid-base balance, working alongside the HCO3− buffer system.
sentences.zh: 钙和磷酸盐 (PO₄³⁻) 在维持血液酸碱平衡方面发挥着重要的支持作用，与 HCO3− 缓冲系统一起发挥作用。

sentences.en: Phosphate can accept or donate hydrogen ions to help stabilize pH, and when blood becomes acidic, calcium and phosphate can be released from bone to help neutralize excess acid (Salcedo-Betancourt & Moe, 2024).
sentences.zh: 磷酸盐可以接受或提供氢离子以帮助稳定 pH 值，当血液变成酸性时，钙和磷酸盐可以从骨骼中释放出来，以帮助中和多余的酸（Salcedo-Betancourt & Moe，2024）。

blocks.html_en: This leads to the question as to whether increases in atmospheric CO2 pose a threat to human health. There are now many studies that review health effects in the range 600–5,000 ppm CO2 (Azuma et al., 2018; Bierwirth, 2025; Carr et al., 2025; Jacobson et al., 2019) although there is still a paucity of studies investigating the effects of long-term exposure at relevant levels of CO2. As such, the U.S. National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) study, which recorded blood chemistry parameters, such as HCO3−, calcium (Ca), and phosphorus (P), from about 7,000 people every two years between 1999 and 2020, provides a unique opportunity to assess potential secular trends in human blood biochemistry that may be a result of a changing air composition.

sentences.en: This leads to the question as to whether increases in atmospheric CO2 pose a threat to human health.
sentences.zh: 这就引出了一个问题：大气中二氧化碳的增加是否会对人类健康构成威胁。

sentences.en: There are now many studies that review health effects in the range 600–5,000 ppm CO2 (Azuma et al., 2018; Bierwirth, 2025; Carr et al., 2025; Jacobson et al., 2019) although there is still a paucity of studies investigating the effects of long-term exposure at relevant levels of CO2.
sentences.zh: 现在有许多研究审查了 600–5,000 ppm CO2 范围内的健康影响（Azuma 等人，2018 年；Bierwirth，2025 年；Carr 等人，2025 年；Jacobson 等人，2019 年），尽管仍然很少有研究调查长期暴露在相关水平的 CO2 的影响。

sentences.en: As such, the U.S. National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) study, which recorded blood chemistry parameters, such as HCO3−, calcium (Ca), and phosphorus (P), from about 7,000 people every two years between 1999 and 2020, provides a unique opportunity to assess potential secular trends in human blood biochemistry that may be a result of a changing air composition.
sentences.zh: 因此，美国国家健康和营养检查调查 (NHANES) 研究在 1999 年至 2020 年间每两年记录约 7,000 人的血液化学参数，如 HCO3−、钙 (Ca) 和磷 (P)，为评估可能因空气成分变化而导致的人类血液生化的潜在长期趋势提供了独特的机会。

blocks.html_en: Atmospheric carbon dioxide throughout human evolution

sentences.en: Atmospheric carbon dioxide throughout human evolution
sentences.zh: 人类进化过程中的大气二氧化碳

blocks.html_en: The ancestor of modern humans is thought to have evolved between 5 and 8 million years ago, with the first Homo sapiens appearing in the fossil record around 150,000 years ago (Wood, 1996). Although not precise, it appears that throughout most, if not all, of the ensuing period of human evolution, levels of CO2 in the atmosphere remained relatively stable around 300 parts per million (ppm). These data were derived from a combination of studies of relict features including air trapped in ice cores (Barnola et al., 1987), the composition of fossil plankton (Zachos et al., 2001) and Carbon-13 (13C) content in fossil plant material (Cui et al., 2020). However, since the advent of widespread industrialisation, atmospheric CO2 levels have exponentially increased (Fig. 1). In just the last ~ 50 years it has risen from < 340 ppm (in 1980), to > 420 ppm in 2025 (Lan et al., 2025). Atmospheric CO2 is currently increasing at more than 2 ppm each year, largely due to humanity’s activities, such as the burning of fossil fuels (Eggleton, 2012).

sentences.en: The ancestor of modern humans is thought to have evolved between 5 and 8 million years ago, with the first Homo sapiens appearing in the fossil record around 150,000 years ago (Wood, 1996).
sentences.zh: 现代人类的祖先被认为是在 5 至 800 万年前进化而来，第一个智人在大约 15 万年前出现在化石记录中（Wood，1996）。

sentences.en: Although not precise, it appears that throughout most, if not all, of the ensuing period of human evolution, levels of CO2 in the atmosphere remained relatively stable around 300 parts per million (ppm).
sentences.zh: 虽然不精确，但似乎在人类进化的随后阶段的大部分（如果不是全部）中，大气中的二氧化碳水平相对稳定地保持在百万分之 300 (ppm) 左右。

sentences.en: These data were derived from a combination of studies of relict features including air trapped in ice cores (Barnola et al., 1987), the composition of fossil plankton (Zachos et al., 2001) and Carbon-13 (13C) content in fossil plant material (Cui et al., 2020).
sentences.zh: 这些数据来源于对遗迹特征的综合研究，包括冰芯中滞留的空气（Barnola 等人，1987 年）、浮游生物化石的组成（Zachos 等人，2001 年）和植物化石材料中的碳 13 (13C) 含量（Cui 等人，2020 年）。

sentences.en: However, since the advent of widespread industrialisation, atmospheric CO2 levels have exponentially increased (Fig. 1).
sentences.zh: 然而，自从广泛的工业化出现以来，大气中的二氧化碳水平呈指数增长（图1）。

sentences.en: In just the last ~ 50 years it has risen from < 340 ppm (in 1980), to > 420 ppm in 2025 (Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: 仅在过去约 50 年里，它就从 < 340 ppm（1980 年）上升到 2025 年 > 420 ppm（Lan 等人，2025 年）。

sentences.en: Atmospheric CO2 is currently increasing at more than 2 ppm each year, largely due to humanity’s activities, such as the burning of fossil fuels (Eggleton, 2012).
sentences.zh: 目前，大气中的二氧化碳含量每年增加超过 2 ppm，这主要是由于人类活动，例如燃烧化石燃料（Eggleton，2012）。

blocks.html_en: Atmospheric carbon dioxide concentrations (in ppm) over the last 800,000 years, based on measurements of air trapped in Antarctic ice (Lüthi et al., 2008; Rubino et al., 2019), and direct measurements made at the Mauna Loa Observatory (1958 to present) (Keeling et al., 2005)

sentences.en: Atmospheric carbon dioxide concentrations (in ppm) over the last 800,000 years, based on measurements of air trapped in Antarctic ice (Lüthi et al., 2008; Rubino et al., 2019), and direct measurements made at the Mauna Loa Observatory (1958 to present) (Keeling et al., 2005)
sentences.zh: 过去 80 万年的大气二氧化碳浓度（以 ppm 为单位），基于对南极冰层中滞留空气的测量（Lüthi 等人，2008 年；Rubino 等人，2019 年）以及莫纳罗亚天文台的直接测量（1958 年至今）（Keeling 等人，2005 年）

blocks.html_en: Temporal trend in human serum bicarbonate, calcium and phosphorus levels

sentences.en: Temporal trend in human serum bicarbonate, calcium and phosphorus levels
sentences.zh: 人血清碳酸氢盐、钙和磷水平的时间趋势

blocks.html_en: As levels of atmospheric CO2 increase, it is obvious that humans will have no option but to inhale more CO2. It is also known that increased blood loading with CO2 (hypercapnia) is physiologically correlated with HCO3− levels (Malte & Wang, 2024), due to the hydration of CO₂ to form carbonic acid, which dissociates into H⁺ and HCO₃⁻. To compensate, renal mechanisms increase HCO₃⁻ reabsorption and generation to buffer H⁺ and partially restore pH homeostasis (Alka & Casey, 2014). This potentially represents a health risk if HCO3− levels increase above the normal healthy range and/or the duration of increased HCO3− is excessive. In healthy adult humans, arterial HCO3− is typically between 22 and 26mEq/L (Larkin & Zimmanck, 2015), although a recent re-evaluation suggests that 22.1–28.3mEq/L is more appropriate for arterial blood, and up to 30mEq/L is appropriate for venous blood (Kraut & Madias, 2018).

sentences.en: As levels of atmospheric CO2 increase, it is obvious that humans will have no option but to inhale more CO2.
sentences.zh: 随着大气中二氧化碳含量的增加，人类显然别无选择，只能吸入更多的二氧化碳。

sentences.en: It is also known that increased blood loading with CO2 (hypercapnia) is physiologically correlated with HCO3− levels (Malte & Wang, 2024), due to the hydration of CO₂ to form carbonic acid, which dissociates into H⁺ and HCO₃⁻.
sentences.zh: 众所周知，CO2 血液负荷增加（高碳酸血症）在生理上与 HCO3− 水平相关（Malte & Wang，2024），因为 CO2 水合形成碳酸，并分解成 H⁺ 和 HCO₃⁻。

sentences.en: To compensate, renal mechanisms increase HCO₃⁻ reabsorption and generation to buffer H⁺ and partially restore pH homeostasis (Alka & Casey, 2014).
sentences.zh: 为了进行补偿，肾脏机制会增加 HCO₃⁻ 的重吸收和生成，以缓冲 H⁺ 并部分恢复 pH 稳态（Alka & Casey，2014）。

sentences.en: This potentially represents a health risk if HCO3− levels increase above the normal healthy range and/or the duration of increased HCO3− is excessive.
sentences.zh: 如果 HCO3− 水平升高至正常健康范围以上和/或 HCO3− 升高的持续时间过长，则可能存在健康风险。

sentences.en: In healthy adult humans, arterial HCO3− is typically between 22 and 26mEq/L (Larkin & Zimmanck, 2015), although a recent re-evaluation suggests that 22.1–28.3mEq/L is more appropriate for arterial blood, and up to 30mEq/L is appropriate for venous blood (Kraut & Madias, 2018).
sentences.zh: 在健康成年人中，动脉 HCO3− 通常在 22 至 26mEq/L 之间（Larkin 和 Zimmanck，2015），尽管最近的重新评估表明 22.1–28.3mEq/L 更适合动脉血，而高达 30mEq/L 更适合静脉血（Kraut 和 Madias，2018）。

blocks.html_en: Although temporal population biochemical data relating to HCO3− are rare, a previous study (Zheutlin et al., 2014) examined data from the U.S. National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) from 1999 to 2012 (7 cycles) looking at the population average HCO3− levels in blood samples from a total of 33,546 adults (~ 5,000 per cycle). Between 1999 and 2012, there was an upward trend in serum HCO3− levels in the study population, with it increasing approximately 5%, from ~ 23.8mEq/L in 1999–2000 to ~ 25.0mEq/L in 2011–2012. This increase paralleled atmospheric CO2 levels which increased by a similar proportion over the same period (from ~ 369ppm to ~ 393ppm) (Lan et al., 2025). The authors question whether “the increasing trend in serum bicarbonate found in our study is related to elevated ambient CO2 and climate change”.

sentences.en: Although temporal population biochemical data relating to HCO3− are rare, a previous study (Zheutlin et al., 2014) examined data from the U.S. National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES) from 1999 to 2012 (7 cycles) looking at the population average HCO3− levels in blood samples from a total of 33,546 adults (~ 5,000 per cycle).
sentences.zh: 尽管与 HCO3− 相关的临时人群生化数据很少，但之前的一项研究（Zheutlin 等人，2014 年）检查了 1999 年至 2012 年（7 个周期）美国国家健康和营养检查调查 (NHANES) 的数据，观察了总共 33,546 名成年人（每个周期约 5,000 个）血液样本中的人群平均 HCO3− 水平。

sentences.en: Between 1999 and 2012, there was an upward trend in serum HCO3− levels in the study population, with it increasing approximately 5%, from ~ 23.8mEq/L in 1999–2000 to ~ 25.0mEq/L in 2011–2012.
sentences.zh: 1999年至2012年间，研究人群的血清HCO3−水平呈上升趋势，增加了约5%，从1999-2000年的约23.8mEq/L增加到2011-2012年的约25.0mEq/L。

sentences.en: This increase paralleled atmospheric CO2 levels which increased by a similar proportion over the same period (from ~ 369ppm to ~ 393ppm) (Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: 这一增加与大气二氧化碳水平平行，同期大气二氧化碳水平也以类似比例增加（从约 369 ppm 至约 393 ppm）（Lan 等人，2025 年）。

sentences.en: The authors question whether “the increasing trend in serum bicarbonate found in our study is related to elevated ambient CO2 and climate change”.
sentences.zh: 作者质疑“我们研究中发现的血清碳酸氢盐的增加趋势是否与环境二氧化碳浓度升高和气候变化有关”。

blocks.html_en: Methods

sentences.en: Methods
sentences.zh: 方法

blocks.html_en: The overarching NHANES protocol is ethically approved by the Ethics Review Committee of the National Center for Health Statistics, which involved obtaining informed consent from all participants. The NHANES project assesses health and nutrition in a representative sample of adults and children in the United States via interviews, physical examinations and laboratory tests.

sentences.en: The overarching NHANES protocol is ethically approved by the Ethics Review Committee of the National Center for Health Statistics, which involved obtaining informed consent from all participants.
sentences.zh: NHANES 的总体方案得到了国家卫生统计中心伦理审查委员会的伦理批准，其中涉及获得所有参与者的知情同意。

sentences.en: The NHANES project assesses health and nutrition in a representative sample of adults and children in the United States via interviews, physical examinations and laboratory tests.
sentences.zh: NHANES 项目通过访谈、体检和实验室测试来评估美国成人和儿童代表性样本的健康和营养状况。

blocks.html_en: In the present study, we extend the 7-cycle trend analysis of Zheutlin et al. (2014) to include more recent and comprehensive NHANES data. Our analyses encompass an additional 4-cycles (up to 2019) and include all records from the NHANES database (i.e. from birth to 80 + years of age; ~7,000 records per cycle). From the database, we extracted data on levels of bicarbonate (mEg/L), calcium (mmol/L) and phosphorus (mmol/L) in participant serum. These data were averaged across all participants for each cycle, giving mean values at each time point. Atmospheric carbon dioxide levels at Mauna Loa, Hawaii over the same time period were obtained the Global Monitoring Laboratory dataset (Lan et al., 2025).

sentences.en: In the present study, we extend the 7-cycle trend analysis of Zheutlin et al. (2014) to include more recent and comprehensive NHANES data.
sentences.zh: 在本研究中，我们扩展了 Zheutlin 等人的 7 周期趋势分析。 (2014) 包括更最新、更全面的 NHANES 数据。

sentences.en: Our analyses encompass an additional 4-cycles (up to 2019) and include all records from the NHANES database (i.e. from birth to 80 + years of age; ~7,000 records per cycle).
sentences.zh: 我们的分析涵盖额外的 4 个周期（截至 2019 年），并包括 NHANES 数据库中的所有记录（即从出生到 80 岁以上；每个周期约 7,000 条记录）。

sentences.en: From the database, we extracted data on levels of bicarbonate (mEg/L), calcium (mmol/L) and phosphorus (mmol/L) in participant serum.
sentences.zh: 我们从数据库中提取了参与者血清中碳酸氢根 (mEg/L)、钙 (mmol/L) 和磷 (mmol/L) 水平的数据。

sentences.en: These data were averaged across all participants for each cycle, giving mean values at each time point.
sentences.zh: 这些数据是每个周期所有参与者的平均值，给出每个时间点的平均值。

sentences.en: Atmospheric carbon dioxide levels at Mauna Loa, Hawaii over the same time period were obtained the Global Monitoring Laboratory dataset (Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: 同一时期夏威夷莫纳罗亚的大气二氧化碳水平由全球监测实验室数据集获得（Lan 等人，2025 年）。

blocks.html_en: Results

sentences.en: Results
sentences.zh: 结果

blocks.html_en: Our key finding was that the increase identified by Zheutlin et al. (2014) has continued (Fig. 2). The most recent serum HCO3− levels measured in the NHANES population (which uses venous blood) was 25.3mEq/L (in 2019–2020), representing a ~ 7% increase from 1999. Similarly, this has paralleled atmospheric CO2 over the same period. As stated above, the upper healthy limit for HCO3− in venous blood can be taken as 30mEq/L (Kraut & Madias, 2018), although this value requires further scrutiny particularly given that the high HCO3− condition would likely be perpetual in the future as atmospheric CO2 continues to increase. Assuming a linear relationship and a ~ 0.34% increase rate per year, the calculated trendline in Fig. 2A predicts that the healthy maximum HCO3− level of 30mEq/L will be reached in the year 2076. Due to the large sample sizes, population level variation in serum HCO3− levels is low (SE based on unweighted data < 0.032 for all years).

sentences.en: Our key finding was that the increase identified by Zheutlin et al. (2014) has continued (Fig. 2).
sentences.zh: 我们的主要发现是 Zheutlin 等人发现的增加。 （2014）继续进行（图2）。

sentences.en: The most recent serum HCO3− levels measured in the NHANES population (which uses venous blood) was 25.3mEq/L (in 2019–2020), representing a ~ 7% increase from 1999.

sentences.en: Similarly, this has paralleled atmospheric CO2 over the same period.
sentences.zh: 同样，这与同一时期大气中的二氧化碳排放量相当。

sentences.en: As stated above, the upper healthy limit for HCO3− in venous blood can be taken as 30mEq/L (Kraut & Madias, 2018), although this value requires further scrutiny particularly given that the high HCO3− condition would likely be perpetual in the future as atmospheric CO2 continues to increase.
sentences.zh: 如上所述，静脉血中 HCO3− 的健康上限可取为 30mEq/L（Kraut & Madias，2018），尽管该值需要进一步审查，特别是考虑到随着大气中二氧化碳的持续增加，未来高 HCO3− 状态可能会永久存在。

sentences.en: Assuming a linear relationship and a ~ 0.34% increase rate per year, the calculated trendline in Fig. 2A predicts that the healthy maximum HCO3− level of 30mEq/L will be reached in the year 2076.
sentences.zh: 假设存在线性关系且每年增长率约为 0.34%，图 2A 中计算的趋势线预测，健康的最大 HCO3− 水平将在 2076 年达到 30mEq/L。

blocks.html_en: (A) Comparison between temporal trend in population serum bicarbonate (filled circles, left y-axis) in U.S. adults from the NHANES biochemistry database and measured atmospheric concentration CO2 (open boxes, dotted line, right y-axis) at Mauna Loa, Hawaii (Lan et al., 2025). The black line is the trendline for HCO3− with the formula y = 0.081x-138.15. (B) Temporal trends in population serum calcium (filled triangles, solid line left y-axis) and phosphorus (open diamonds, dotted line, right y-axis) in U.S. adults from the NHANES biochemistry database over the same period of time

sentences.en: (A) Comparison between temporal trend in population serum bicarbonate (filled circles, left y-axis) in U.S. adults from the NHANES biochemistry database and measured atmospheric concentration CO2 (open boxes, dotted line, right y-axis) at Mauna Loa, Hawaii (Lan et al., 2025).
sentences.zh: (A) NHANES 生物化学数据库中美国成年人人群血清碳酸氢盐（实心圆圈，左 y 轴）的时间趋势与夏威夷莫纳罗亚山测量的大气 CO2 浓度（空心框，虚线，右 y 轴）之间的比较（Lan 等人，2025 年）。

sentences.zh: 黑线是 HCO3− 的趋势线，公式为 y = 0.081x-138.15。

sentences.en: (B) Temporal trends in population serum calcium (filled triangles, solid line left y-axis) and phosphorus (open diamonds, dotted line, right y-axis) in U.S. adults from the NHANES biochemistry database over the same period of time
sentences.zh: (B) 来自 NHANES 生物化学数据库的美国成年人同期人群血清钙（实心三角形，实线左侧 y 轴）和磷（空心菱形，虚线，右侧 y 轴）的时间趋势

blocks.html_en: Similarly, Fig. 2B illustrates changes in serum calcium (Ca) and phosphorus (P) levels in the same population between 1999 and 2020. Mean serum Ca levels have decreased ~ 2% over this period, while P levels have decreased by ~ 7% (if the potentially spurious measurement in NHANES 1999–2000 is excluded). Total serum Ca levels for healthy adults are generally accepted to be between 2.1 and 2.6 mmol/L, with levels below this termed hypocalcemia (Bazydlo et al., 2014). For P the healthy range is 0.81 to 1.45 mmol/L (Bazydlo et al., 2014), below which hypophosphatemia occurs. Like serum HCO3−, due to the large sample sizes, population level variation in both Ca and P are low (SE based on unweighted data < 0.0012 for Ca and < 0.0027 for P for all years). If we also assume linear relationships for these parameters, the calculated trendlines in Fig. 2B predict that the lower limits for Ca and P would be reached in the years 2099 and 2085 respectively. These are clearly estimations given the variability in the data. As with bicarbonate, these thresholds could be reached earlier as atmospheric CO2 levels continue to increase.

sentences.en: Similarly, Fig. 2B illustrates changes in serum calcium (Ca) and phosphorus (P) levels in the same population between 1999 and 2020.
sentences.zh: 同样，图2B显示了1999年至2020年间同一人群血清钙（Ca）和磷（P）水平的变化。

sentences.en: Mean serum Ca levels have decreased ~ 2% over this period, while P levels have decreased by ~ 7% (if the potentially spurious measurement in NHANES 1999–2000 is excluded).

sentences.en: Total serum Ca levels for healthy adults are generally accepted to be between 2.1 and 2.6 mmol/L, with levels below this termed hypocalcemia (Bazydlo et al., 2014).
sentences.zh: 健康成人的血清总钙水平通常被认为在 2.1 至 2.6 mmol/L 之间，低于此水平称为低钙血症（Bazydlo 等，2014）。

sentences.zh: P 的健康范围是 0.81 至 1.45 mmol/L（Bazydlo 等，2014），低于该范围会发生低磷血症。

sentences.en: Like serum HCO3−, due to the large sample sizes, population level variation in both Ca and P are low (SE based on unweighted data < 0.0012 for Ca and < 0.0027 for P for all years).
sentences.zh: 与血清 HCO3− 一样，由于样本量较大，Ca 和 P 的总体水平变化较低（SE 基于所有年份的未加权数据，Ca < 0.0012，P < 0.0027）。

sentences.en: If we also assume linear relationships for these parameters, the calculated trendlines in Fig. 2B predict that the lower limits for Ca and P would be reached in the years 2099 and 2085 respectively.

blocks.html_en: Discussion

sentences.en: Discussion
sentences.zh: 讨论

blocks.html_en: The trends evident in NHANES data indicate that human physiology has progressively and consistently altered over the last ~ 25 years. These changes are consistent with effects that might occur due to breathing increasing levels of atmospheric CO2. Under normal circumstances CO2 is excreted from the body via breathing with the ventilation rate determined by chemoreceptors measuring blood pH (Eckenhoff & Longnecker, 1995). This is likely a system that has been tuned to the usually stable level of atmospheric CO2 at around 280 ppm throughout our evolution (Fig. 1).

sentences.en: The trends evident in NHANES data indicate that human physiology has progressively and consistently altered over the last ~ 25 years.
sentences.zh: NHANES 数据中明显的趋势表明，人类生理机能在过去约 25 年中不断发生逐步变化。

sentences.en: These changes are consistent with effects that might occur due to breathing increasing levels of atmospheric CO2.
sentences.zh: 这些变化与呼吸大气中二氧化碳含量增加可能产生的影响是一致的。

sentences.en: Under normal circumstances CO2 is excreted from the body via breathing with the ventilation rate determined by chemoreceptors measuring blood pH (Eckenhoff & Longnecker, 1995).
sentences.zh: 在正常情况下，二氧化碳通过呼吸从体内排出，通气率由测量血液 pH 值的化学感受器确定（Eckenhoff 和 Longnecker，1995）。

sentences.en: This is likely a system that has been tuned to the usually stable level of atmospheric CO2 at around 280 ppm throughout our evolution (Fig. 1).
sentences.zh: 这很可能是一个在整个演化过程中大气二氧化碳浓度通常稳定在 280 ppm 左右的系统（图 1）。

blocks.html_en: In situations of excess CO2, the bones provide an early compensation mechanism by storing CO2 as bicarbonate and carbonate (Schaefer et al., 1979a; Stumm, 2023). Additionally bone resorption may occur (Holy et al., 2012), whereby minerals such as Ca and phosphate (PO₄³⁻) are released from the bones into the blood, thereby temporarily increasing blood pH (Arnett, 2010). Studies of long-term exposure to 5,000 ppm CO2 in guinea pigs show that blood levels of Ca and P are cyclic after CO2 exposure (Schaefer et al., 1979a). When bone (and kidney) Ca increase, blood Ca decreases. Ca and P levels are mirrored. In chronic acidosis, however, the ability of the kidneys to handle Ca is impaired, such that there may be an increase in Ca excretion and a net loss of Ca from the body in urine (hypercalciuria) over time (Bushinsky et al., 2003). Schaefer (1982) determined that after CO2 exposure, an initial decline in blood calcium corresponds to a decline in blood pH and marks a period of deposition of CO2 in bones at 1.5% CO2 (Schaefer, 1982). At 7,000 ppm CO2, human blood levels of Ca and P were seen to rise after 20 days (Holy et al., 2012) with loss of these elements from the bones. At 1.5% CO2, plasma calcium, mirroring the changes in pH, showed a decrease during the first 23 days of exposure and a return to initial levels during the latter part of exposure (Schaefer et al., 1963). The significant decrease in plasma calcium during this period of CO2 retention suggests that Ca was deposited in the skeleton together with CO2 (Schaefer et al. 1963). Thus, the effects of breathing elevated CO2 for extended periods on serum levels of calcium and phosphorus/phosphates is complex. This is further complicated by most studies being conducted at higher CO2 levels than the current atmosphere and involving acidic (low pH) conditions. However, a recent study provides some insight by demonstrating that, at near neutral pH, excess HCO3− ions can become incorporated in precipitating amorphous calcium carbonate (Huang et al., 2021). It appears that increasing HCO3− in the blood, as shown by the NHANES data, is involved in the exchange of H+ with Ca and P and the subsequent storage in the bones of CO2 via calcium carbonate and phosphates. This is a potential reason for declining calcium and phosphate levels in the blood.

sentences.en: In situations of excess CO2, the bones provide an early compensation mechanism by storing CO2 as bicarbonate and carbonate (Schaefer et al., 1979a; Stumm, 2023).
sentences.zh: 在二氧化碳过量的情况下，骨骼通过将二氧化碳储存为碳酸氢盐和碳酸盐来提供早期补偿机制（Schaefer 等人，1979a；Stumm，2023）。

sentences.en: Additionally bone resorption may occur (Holy et al., 2012), whereby minerals such as Ca and phosphate (PO₄³⁻) are released from the bones into the blood, thereby temporarily increasing blood pH (Arnett, 2010).
sentences.zh: 此外，可能会发生骨吸收（Holy et al., 2012），钙和磷酸盐（PO₄3⁻）等矿物质从骨骼中释放到血液中，从而暂时增加血液 pH 值（Arnett, 2010）。

sentences.en: Studies of long-term exposure to 5,000 ppm CO2 in guinea pigs show that blood levels of Ca and P are cyclic after CO2 exposure (Schaefer et al., 1979a).
sentences.zh: 对豚鼠长期暴露于 5,000 ppm CO2 的研究表明，暴露于 CO2 后血液中 Ca 和 P 的水平呈周期性变化（Schaefer 等，1979a）。

sentences.en: When bone (and kidney) Ca increase, blood Ca decreases.
sentences.zh: 当骨（和肾）Ca 增加时，血Ca 减少。

sentences.en: Ca and P levels are mirrored.
sentences.zh: Ca 和 P 水平是镜像的。

sentences.en: In chronic acidosis, however, the ability of the kidneys to handle Ca is impaired, such that there may be an increase in Ca excretion and a net loss of Ca from the body in urine (hypercalciuria) over time (Bushinsky et al., 2003).
sentences.zh: 然而，在慢性酸中毒中，肾脏处理钙的能力受到损害，因此随着时间的推移，钙的排泄可能会增加，并且尿液中的钙净损失（高钙尿症）（Bushinsky et al., 2003）。

sentences.en: Schaefer (1982) determined that after CO2 exposure, an initial decline in blood calcium corresponds to a decline in blood pH and marks a period of deposition of CO2 in bones at 1.5% CO2 (Schaefer, 1982).
sentences.zh: Schaefer (1982) 确定，接触 CO2 后，血钙的最初下降对应于血液 pH 值的下降，并标志着 CO2 在 1.5% CO2 下在骨骼中沉积的时期 (Schaefer, 1982)。

sentences.en: At 7,000 ppm CO2, human blood levels of Ca and P were seen to rise after 20 days (Holy et al., 2012) with loss of these elements from the bones.
sentences.zh: 在 7,000 ppm 二氧化碳浓度下，20 天后，人体血液中的钙和磷含量会上升（Holy 等人，2012 年），因为这些元素会从骨骼中流失。

sentences.en: At 1.5% CO2, plasma calcium, mirroring the changes in pH, showed a decrease during the first 23 days of exposure and a return to initial levels during the latter part of exposure (Schaefer et al., 1963).
sentences.zh: 在 1.5% CO2 条件下，血浆钙（反映 pH 值的变化）在暴露的前 23 天内表现出下降，并在暴露的后半段恢复到初始水平（Schaefer 等，1963）。

sentences.en: The significant decrease in plasma calcium during this period of CO2 retention suggests that Ca was deposited in the skeleton together with CO2 (Schaefer et al. 1963).
sentences.zh: 在 CO2 滞留期间，血浆钙显着下降，表明 Ca 与 CO2 一起沉积在骨骼中（Schaefer 等，1963）。

sentences.en: Thus, the effects of breathing elevated CO2 for extended periods on serum levels of calcium and phosphorus/phosphates is complex.
sentences.zh: 因此，长时间呼吸升高的二氧化碳对钙和磷/磷酸盐的血清水平的影响是复杂的。

sentences.en: This is further complicated by most studies being conducted at higher CO2 levels than the current atmosphere and involving acidic (low pH) conditions.
sentences.zh: 由于大多数研究是在比当前大气更高的二氧化碳浓度下进行的，并且涉及酸性（低 pH）条件，这使得情况变得更加复杂。

sentences.en: However, a recent study provides some insight by demonstrating that, at near neutral pH, excess HCO3− ions can become incorporated in precipitating amorphous calcium carbonate (Huang et al., 2021).
sentences.zh: 然而，最近的一项研究提供了一些见解，证明在接近中性的 pH 值下，过量的 HCO3− 离子可能会并入沉淀的无定形碳酸钙中（Huang 等人，2021）。

sentences.en: It appears that increasing HCO3− in the blood, as shown by the NHANES data, is involved in the exchange of H+ with Ca and P and the subsequent storage in the bones of CO2 via calcium carbonate and phosphates.
sentences.zh: 正如 NHANES 数据所示，血液中 HCO3− 的增加似乎参与了 H+ 与 Ca 和 P 的交换，以及随后通过碳酸钙和磷酸盐在骨骼中储存 CO2。

sentences.en: This is a potential reason for declining calcium and phosphate levels in the blood.
sentences.zh: 这是血液中钙和磷酸盐水平下降的潜在原因。

blocks.html_en: Whatever the exact mechanisms, the changes seen in human blood chemistry over the last quarter of a century, as illustrated by the NHANES data, are greatly concerning from a human health perspective. They show we may have already entered a period where there is permanent and developing physiological compensation for CO2 in our bodies.

sentences.en: Whatever the exact mechanisms, the changes seen in human blood chemistry over the last quarter of a century, as illustrated by the NHANES data, are greatly concerning from a human health perspective.
sentences.zh: 无论确切的机制如何，如 NHANES 数据所示，过去 25 年人类血液化学变化从人类健康的角度来看是非常令人担忧的。

sentences.en: They show we may have already entered a period where there is permanent and developing physiological compensation for CO2 in our bodies.
sentences.zh: 他们表明，我们可能已经进入了一个时期，我们体内的二氧化碳存在永久性的、正在发展的生理补偿。

blocks.html_en: Health effects of long-term increases in bicarbonate and carbon dioxide

sentences.en: Health effects of long-term increases in bicarbonate and carbon dioxide
sentences.zh: 碳酸氢盐和二氧化碳长期增加对健康的影响

blocks.html_en: As already stated, HCO3− is a critical physiological anion that plays crucial roles in the transport of CO2 throughout the body and in buffering pH (Sherwood, 2013). The fact that its level in the blood is increasing (at a population scale) in parallel with increasing atmospheric CO2 is concerning. The question remains, however, whether this is really a problem, or whether human physiology will not be negatively impacted. Indeed, some authors have elegantly argued that blood acid-base compensatory mechanisms will easily be able to handle further increases in atmospheric CO2 (Malte & Wang, 2024; Saunders & Habgood, 2023). Unfortunately, these arguments ignore the mounting evidence that even small, short-term increases in atmospheric CO2 do indeed impact physiology in many species ranging from gastropods (Mardones et al., 2022; Navarro et al., 2022), to rodents (Kiray et al., 2014; Larcombe et al., 2021; Martrette et al., 2017; Wyrwoll et al., 2022) and humans (Allen et al., 2016; Lu et al., 2015; Satish et al., 2012; Seppänen et al., 1999). Additionally, they do not consider the impact of longer-term (i.e. life-course) exposure, or exposure during critical windows of development and the potential for such exposures to alter our physiological compensation mechanisms. To the best of our knowledge, currently there are no studies of long-term (approximating lifetime) health effects of exposure to atmospheric CO2 in the range that is vitally important for the near future (500–800 ppm) (IPCC, 2022). However, two recent publications from our research group explore the effects of “life-time” (in utero to early adulthood) exposure to levels just above this (890 ppm) using a mouse model (Larcombe et al., 2021; Wyrwoll et al., 2022). We measured effects on lung structure and function, behaviour and neural expression of genes in CO2 exposed groups.

sentences.en: As already stated, HCO3− is a critical physiological anion that plays crucial roles in the transport of CO2 throughout the body and in buffering pH (Sherwood, 2013).
sentences.zh: 如前所述，HCO3− 是一种重要的生理阴离子，在 CO2 输送至全身和缓冲 pH 方面发挥着至关重要的作用（Sherwood，2013）。

sentences.en: The fact that its level in the blood is increasing (at a population scale) in parallel with increasing atmospheric CO2 is concerning.
sentences.zh: 事实上，它在血液中的水平（在人口规模上）随着大气中二氧化碳的增加而增加，这一事实令人担忧。

sentences.en: The question remains, however, whether this is really a problem, or whether human physiology will not be negatively impacted.
sentences.zh: 然而，问题仍然在于，这是否真的是一个问题，或者人类生理学是否不会受到负面影响。

sentences.en: Indeed, some authors have elegantly argued that blood acid-base compensatory mechanisms will easily be able to handle further increases in atmospheric CO2 (Malte & Wang, 2024; Saunders & Habgood, 2023).
sentences.zh: 事实上，一些作者优雅地认为，血液酸碱补偿机制将能够轻松应对大气中二氧化碳的进一步增加（Malte & Wang，2024；Saunders & Habgood，2023）。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, these arguments ignore the mounting evidence that even small, short-term increases in atmospheric CO2 do indeed impact physiology in many species ranging from gastropods (Mardones et al., 2022; Navarro et al., 2022), to rodents (Kiray et al., 2014; Larcombe et al., 2021; Martrette et al., 2017; Wyrwoll et al., 2022) and humans (Allen et al., 2016; Lu et al., 2015; Satish et al., 2012; Seppänen et al., 1999).
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，这些论点忽略了越来越多的证据，即即使是大气中二氧化碳的短期小幅增加，也确实会影响许多物种的生理机能，从腹足动物（Mardones等，2022；Navarro等，2022）到啮齿动物（Kiray等，2014；Larcombe等，2021；Martrette等，2017；Wyrwoll等， 2022）和人类（Allen 等人，2016；Lu 等人，2015；Satish 等人，2012；Seppänen 等人，1999）。

sentences.en: Additionally, they do not consider the impact of longer-term (i.e. life-course) exposure, or exposure during critical windows of development and the potential for such exposures to alter our physiological compensation mechanisms.
sentences.zh: 此外，他们没有考虑长期（即生命全程）暴露或关键发育窗口期间暴露的影响以及此类暴露改变我们的生理补偿机制的潜力。

sentences.en: To the best of our knowledge, currently there are no studies of long-term (approximating lifetime) health effects of exposure to atmospheric CO2 in the range that is vitally important for the near future (500–800 ppm) (IPCC, 2022).
sentences.zh: 据我们所知，目前还没有关于暴露于对不久的将来至关重要的范围内的大气二氧化碳（500-800 ppm）（500-800 ppm）的长期（大约一生）健康影响的研究（IPCC，2022）。

sentences.en: However, two recent publications from our research group explore the effects of “life-time” (in utero to early adulthood) exposure to levels just above this (890 ppm) using a mouse model (Larcombe et al., 2021; Wyrwoll et al., 2022).
sentences.zh: 然而，我们研究小组最近发表的两篇出版物使用小鼠模型探讨了“一生”（从子宫内到成年早期）暴露于略高于此水平（890 ppm）的影响（Larcombe 等人，2021 年；Wyrwoll 等人，2022 年）。

sentences.en: We measured effects on lung structure and function, behaviour and neural expression of genes in CO2 exposed groups.
sentences.zh: 我们测量了二氧化碳暴露组中基因对肺结构和功能、行为和神经表达的影响。

blocks.html_en: The possible effects of increased HCO3− and CO2 can also potentially be observed from other studies of short-term exposure to slightly higher CO2 levels, such as those commonly experienced in indoor environments (e.g. 1,000 to 3,000 ppm CO2) (reviewed in (Azuma et al., 2017)). For example, an older study (Eliseeva, 1964) reported marked changes in human respiration, circulation, and cerebral electrical activity at 1,000 ppm CO2, while more recent studies of humans and animals have shown harmful effects of CO2 exposure at these levels, such as changes in heart rate, kidney calcification, oxidative stress, neural damage and inflammation (Kiray et al., 2014; MacNaughton et al., 2016; Schaefer, 1982; Thom et al., 2017; Vehviläinen et al., 2016), reviewed in (Jacobson et al., 2019). This is important due to many human populations spending a significant proportion of their time indoors, where CO2 levels are typically higher. For example, The National Human Activity Pattern Survey (NHAPS) found that Americans spent an average of 87% of their time in enclosed buildings and ~ 6% of their time in enclosed vehicles in 1992–1994 (Klepeis et al., 2001). This has potentially increased since the COVID-19 pandemic (Young et al., 2024), with many people working-from-home in environments with poor mechanical air-ventilation (Nazaroff, 2021). The proportion of time spent in indoor, CO2 enriched environments could also contribute to the changes in blood chemistry noted in the NHANES data and is an important consideration in interpreting changes over time, and forecasts for the future.

sentences.en: The possible effects of increased HCO3− and CO2 can also potentially be observed from other studies of short-term exposure to slightly higher CO2 levels, such as those commonly experienced in indoor environments (e.g. 1,000 to 3,000 ppm CO2) (reviewed in (Azuma et al., 2017)).
sentences.zh: HCO3− 和 CO2 增加的可能影响也可以从短期暴露于稍高 CO2 水平的其他研究中观察到，例如室内环境中常见的情况（例如 1,000 至 3,000 ppm CO2）（综述见（Azuma 等人，2017 年））。

sentences.en: For example, an older study (Eliseeva, 1964) reported marked changes in human respiration, circulation, and cerebral electrical activity at 1,000 ppm CO2, while more recent studies of humans and animals have shown harmful effects of CO2 exposure at these levels, such as changes in heart rate, kidney calcification, oxidative stress, neural damage and inflammation (Kiray et al., 2014; MacNaughton et al., 2016; Schaefer, 1982; Thom et al., 2017; Vehviläinen et al., 2016), reviewed in (Jacobson et al., 2019).
sentences.zh: 例如，一项较早的研究（Eliseeva，1964）报告了在 1,000 ppm 二氧化碳浓度下，人类呼吸、循环和脑电活动发生显着变化，而最近对人类和动物的研究表明，在这些浓度下接触二氧化碳会产生有害影响，例如心率变化、肾脏钙化、氧化应激、神经损伤和炎症（Kiray 等人，2014 年；MacNaughton 等人，2016 年；Schaefer， 1982 年；Thom 等人，2017 年；Vehviläinen 等人，2016 年），综述于（Jacobson 等人，2019 年）。

sentences.en: This is important due to many human populations spending a significant proportion of their time indoors, where CO2 levels are typically higher.
sentences.zh: 这一点很重要，因为许多人大部分时间都呆在室内，而室内的二氧化碳含量通常较高。

sentences.en: For example, The National Human Activity Pattern Survey (NHAPS) found that Americans spent an average of 87% of their time in enclosed buildings and ~ 6% of their time in enclosed vehicles in 1992–1994 (Klepeis et al., 2001).
sentences.zh: 例如，国家人类活动模式调查 (NHAPS) 发现，1992 年至 1994 年间，美国人平均有 87% 的时间在封闭式建筑中度过，大约 6% 的时间在封闭式车辆中度过（Klepeis 等，2001）。

sentences.en: This has potentially increased since the COVID-19 pandemic (Young et al., 2024), with many people working-from-home in environments with poor mechanical air-ventilation (Nazaroff, 2021).
sentences.zh: 自 COVID-19 大流行以来，这种情况可能有所增加（Young 等人，2024 年），许多人在机械通风不良的环境中在家工作（Nazaroff，2021 年）。

sentences.en: The proportion of time spent in indoor, CO2 enriched environments could also contribute to the changes in blood chemistry noted in the NHANES data and is an important consideration in interpreting changes over time, and forecasts for the future.
sentences.zh: 在室内、富含二氧化碳的环境中度过的时间比例也可能导致 NHANES 数据中记录的血液化学变化，并且是解释随时间变化和预测未来的重要考虑因素。

blocks.html_en: In animals, CO2 exposure has been found to play a role in oxidative stress caused by reactive oxygen species (ROS) (Ezraty et al., 2011; Kiray et al., 2014). For example. Ezraty et al. (2011) demonstrated that current, and slightly elevated, atmospheric CO2 levels play a role in exacerbating oxidative stress in a bacterial model (Ezraty et al., 2011). Kiray et al. (2014) concluded that 1,000 ppm CO2 is associated with oxidative stress and oxidative damage to brain tissue in mice (Kiray et al., 2014). ROS are produced by aerobic metabolism of molecular oxygen and play a major role in various clinical conditions including malignant diseases, diabetes, atherosclerosis, chronic inflammation and neurological disorders such as Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s diseases (Waris & Ahsan, 2006). In particular, oxidative damage to cellular DNA can lead to mutations resulting in the initiation and progression of cancer.

sentences.en: In animals, CO2 exposure has been found to play a role in oxidative stress caused by reactive oxygen species (ROS) (Ezraty et al., 2011; Kiray et al., 2014).
sentences.zh: 在动物中，已发现二氧化碳暴露在活性氧 (ROS) 引起的氧化应激中发挥作用（Ezraty 等人，2011 年；Kiray 等人，2014 年）。

sentences.en: For example.
sentences.zh: 例如。

sentences.en: Ezraty et al. (2011) demonstrated that current, and slightly elevated, atmospheric CO2 levels play a role in exacerbating oxidative stress in a bacterial model (Ezraty et al., 2011).
sentences.zh: 埃兹拉蒂等人。 (2011) 证明，当前大气中二氧化碳水平略有升高，会加剧细菌模型中的氧化应激 (Ezraty 等，2011)。

sentences.en: Kiray et al. (2014) concluded that 1,000 ppm CO2 is associated with oxidative stress and oxidative damage to brain tissue in mice (Kiray et al., 2014).
sentences.zh: 基拉伊等人。 (2014) 得出结论，1,000 ppm CO2 与小鼠脑组织的氧化应激和氧化损伤有关（Kiray 等，2014）。

sentences.en: ROS are produced by aerobic metabolism of molecular oxygen and play a major role in various clinical conditions including malignant diseases, diabetes, atherosclerosis, chronic inflammation and neurological disorders such as Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s diseases (Waris & Ahsan, 2006).
sentences.zh: ROS是由分子氧的有氧代谢产生的，在各种临床病症中发挥着重要作用，包括恶性疾病、糖尿病、动脉粥样硬化、慢性炎症和神经系统疾病，如帕金森病和阿尔茨海默病（Waris & Ahsan，2006）。

sentences.en: In particular, oxidative damage to cellular DNA can lead to mutations resulting in the initiation and progression of cancer.
sentences.zh: 特别是，细胞 DNA 的氧化损伤可能导致突变，从而导致癌症的发生和进展。

blocks.html_en: As discussed above, increased exposure to CO2 leads to increased blood acidity, and as part of the body’s compensation mechanisms, the kidneys retain bicarbonate helping to normalise blood pH (Schaefer et al., 1979b). This clearly means that bicarbonate levels are normally intimately tied to processes that rely on acid-base balance. However, with long-term high levels of CO2 in the blood, compensation mechanisms are no longer sufficient, metabolic acidosis occurs and the kidneys do not respond in producing bicarbonate. Under these conditions it appears that Ca2+ ions are mobilized to replace H+ ions, producing calcification (CaCO3) of the kidneys and other body tissues such as arteries (Schaefer, 1982). Tissue calcification has been observed in the kidneys of guinea pigs and rats exposed to 1,500 ppm CO2 for ~ 6 to ~ 15 weeks (Schaefer et al., 1979b). The effect may be driven by the over-expression of the carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme caused by having more CO2 to catalyse (Phelan et al., 2021) since high CA activity is associated with calcification (Adeva-Andany et al., 2014). This is a protein malfunction that appears possible at projected future CO2 levels given lifetime exposure. CA malfunction due to elevated CO2 and HCO3− can also have indirect effects on the development and progression of diseases such as cancer and diabetes (Aspatwar et al., 2021). Similarly, a recent report suggests that exposure to increasing atmospheric CO2 “is likely to lead to proteome malfunction” – due to “protein misfolding, aggregation, charge distribution, and altered interaction with other molecules” (Duarte et al., 2020). The authors suggest that these changes could help explain the increasing prevalence of certain syndromes including diabetes and neurological disorders. In a similar vein, Kryvenko and Vadász (2021) describe how acute and chronic hypercapnia can impair endoplasmic reticulum (ER) function. The ER is a cellular organelle that serves many roles including protein synthesis and calcium storage (Voeltz et al., 2002). Elevated HCO3− and CO2 levels are thought to cause ER stress, which alters ER protein-folding homeostasis potentially leading to tissue and organ malfunction (Kryvenko & Vadász, 2021). More research is required to understand the potential for slightly elevated atmospheric CO2 levels to lead to these changes.

sentences.en: As discussed above, increased exposure to CO2 leads to increased blood acidity, and as part of the body’s compensation mechanisms, the kidneys retain bicarbonate helping to normalise blood pH (Schaefer et al., 1979b).
sentences.zh: 如上所述，增加接触 CO2 会导致血液酸度增加，作为身体补偿机制的一部分，肾脏保留碳酸氢盐，有助于使血液 pH 值正常化（Schaefer 等，1979b）。

sentences.en: This clearly means that bicarbonate levels are normally intimately tied to processes that rely on acid-base balance.
sentences.zh: 这显然意味着碳酸氢盐水平通常与依赖酸碱平衡的过程密切相关。

sentences.en: However, with long-term high levels of CO2 in the blood, compensation mechanisms are no longer sufficient, metabolic acidosis occurs and the kidneys do not respond in producing bicarbonate.
sentences.zh: 然而，随着血液中长期高水平的二氧化碳，补偿机制不再充分，发生代谢性酸中毒，肾脏无法产生碳酸氢盐。

sentences.en: Under these conditions it appears that Ca2+ ions are mobilized to replace H+ ions, producing calcification (CaCO3) of the kidneys and other body tissues such as arteries (Schaefer, 1982).
sentences.zh: 在这些条件下，Ca2+ 离子似乎被动员起来取代 H+ 离子，从而产生肾脏和动脉等其他身体组织的钙化 (CaCO3) (Schaefer, 1982)。

sentences.en: Tissue calcification has been observed in the kidneys of guinea pigs and rats exposed to 1,500 ppm CO2 for ~ 6 to ~ 15 weeks (Schaefer et al., 1979b).
sentences.zh: 在暴露于 1,500 ppm CO2 约 6 至约 15 周的豚鼠和大鼠的肾脏中观察到组织钙化（Schaefer 等人，1979b）。

sentences.en: The effect may be driven by the over-expression of the carbonic anhydrase (CA) enzyme caused by having more CO2 to catalyse (Phelan et al., 2021) since high CA activity is associated with calcification (Adeva-Andany et al., 2014).
sentences.zh: 该效应可能是由于有更多二氧化碳催化而导致碳酸酐酶 (CA) 过度表达 (Phelan 等人，2021)，因为高 CA 活性与钙化相关 (Adeva-Andany 等人，2014)。

sentences.en: This is a protein malfunction that appears possible at projected future CO2 levels given lifetime exposure.
sentences.zh: 这是一种蛋白质故障，在考虑到终生接触的预计未来二氧化碳水平下，这种蛋白质故障似乎是可能的。

sentences.en: CA malfunction due to elevated CO2 and HCO3− can also have indirect effects on the development and progression of diseases such as cancer and diabetes (Aspatwar et al., 2021).
sentences.zh: CO2 和 HCO3− 升高导致的 CA 功能障碍也会对癌症和糖尿病等疾病的发生和进展产生间接影响 (Aspatwar et al., 2021)。

sentences.en: Similarly, a recent report suggests that exposure to increasing atmospheric CO2 “is likely to lead to proteome malfunction” – due to “protein misfolding, aggregation, charge distribution, and altered interaction with other molecules” (Duarte et al., 2020).
sentences.zh: 同样，最近的一份报告表明，暴露于不断增加的大气二氧化碳“可能会导致蛋白质组功能障碍”——由于“蛋白质错误折叠、聚集、电荷分布以及与其他分子相互作用的改变”（Duarte 等人，2020）。

sentences.en: The authors suggest that these changes could help explain the increasing prevalence of certain syndromes including diabetes and neurological disorders.
sentences.zh: 作者认为，这些变化可能有助于解释某些综合征（包括糖尿病和神经系统疾病）日益流行的原因。

sentences.en: In a similar vein, Kryvenko and Vadász (2021) describe how acute and chronic hypercapnia can impair endoplasmic reticulum (ER) function.
sentences.zh: 同样，Kryvenko 和 Vadász (2021) 描述了急性和慢性高碳酸血症如何损害内质网 (ER) 功能。

sentences.en: The ER is a cellular organelle that serves many roles including protein synthesis and calcium storage (Voeltz et al., 2002).
sentences.zh: ER 是一种细胞器，具有多种作用，包括蛋白质合成和钙储存（Voeltz 等，2002）。

sentences.en: Elevated HCO3− and CO2 levels are thought to cause ER stress, which alters ER protein-folding homeostasis potentially leading to tissue and organ malfunction (Kryvenko & Vadász, 2021).
sentences.zh: HCO3− 和 CO2 水平升高被认为会导致内质网应激，从而改变内质网蛋白折叠稳态，可能导致组织和器官功能障碍 (Kryvenko & Vadász, 2021)。

sentences.en: More research is required to understand the potential for slightly elevated atmospheric CO2 levels to lead to these changes.
sentences.zh: 需要更多的研究来了解大气二氧化碳水平略微升高导致这些变化的可能性。

blocks.html_en: Another physiological effect of exposure to slightly elevated atmospheric CO2 is its potential to detrimentally impact learning, cognitive abilities and mental health in humans (Allen et al., 2019; Allen et al., 2016; Satish et al., 2012; Scully et al., 2019). There is now a considerable body of published data showing impacts at levels < 1,000ppm CO2, although the effects of exposure remain controversial. For example, one study found no impact of exposure to levels up to 15,000 ppm (Rodeheffer et al., 2018), however the study population was a group of highly trained US Navy submariners. Conversely, studies in young adults (Satish et al., 2012), office workers (Allen et al., 2016) and university staff/students (Snow et al., 2019) showed negative effects at CO2 levels as low as 950 ppm. Of potential importance, the study by Snow et al. (2019), found that there were no clear physiological drivers underlying the measured impacts on cognitive performance. Such studies are supported by assessment of CO2-induced changes in human brainwaves, measured by electroencephalography (EEG) combined with cognitive tests (reviewed in (Zhang et al., 2024)). Such studies show that exposure to CO2 between 1,000 and 2,500 ppm results in heightened brain activity. Although the mechanisms underlying these impacts are not clear, Stumm (2023) postulates that CO2 induced increase in extracellular calcium ions may restrict the movement of sodium ions thereby reducing neuron excitability (Stumm, 2023). Another explanation may be that CO2 signalling activates the autonomic nervous system causing stress which affects cognitive performance (Azuma et al., 2018). Brain activity holds paramount importance for human functioning, acting as the central regulator that coordinates various bodily functions and cognitive processes (Zhang et al., 2024). Given that CO2 exposure at moderate levels appears to prove detrimental to brain activity, additional research is urgently required to assess whether life-time exposure to CO2 levels predicted by climate change modelling will have significant impacts on human cognitive function.

sentences.en: Another physiological effect of exposure to slightly elevated atmospheric CO2 is its potential to detrimentally impact learning, cognitive abilities and mental health in humans (Allen et al., 2019; Allen et al., 2016; Satish et al., 2012; Scully et al., 2019).
sentences.zh: 暴露于稍微升高的大气二氧化碳的另一个生理影响是，它可能对人类的学习、认知能力和心理健康产生不利影响（Allen 等人，2019 年；Allen 等人，2016 年；Satish 等人，2012 年；Scully 等人，2019 年）。

sentences.en: There is now a considerable body of published data showing impacts at levels < 1,000ppm CO2, although the effects of exposure remain controversial.
sentences.zh: 尽管暴露的影响仍然存在争议，但现在有大量已发表的数据显示二氧化碳浓度低于 1,000 ppm 时的影响。

sentences.en: For example, one study found no impact of exposure to levels up to 15,000 ppm (Rodeheffer et al., 2018), however the study population was a group of highly trained US Navy submariners.
sentences.zh: 例如，一项研究发现，暴露于浓度高达 15,000 ppm 的环境中不会产生任何影响（Rodeheffer 等人，2018 年），但研究人群是一群训练有素的美国海军潜艇兵。

sentences.en: Conversely, studies in young adults (Satish et al., 2012), office workers (Allen et al., 2016) and university staff/students (Snow et al., 2019) showed negative effects at CO2 levels as low as 950 ppm.
sentences.zh: 相反，对年轻人（Satish 等人，2012 年）、办公室工作人员（Allen 等人，2016 年）和大学教职员工/学生（Snow 等人，2019 年）的研究表明，二氧化碳水平低至 950 ppm 时会产生负面影响。

sentences.en: Of potential importance, the study by Snow et al. (2019), found that there were no clear physiological drivers underlying the measured impacts on cognitive performance.
sentences.zh: Snow 等人的研究具有潜在的重要性。 (2019) 发现，测量到的对认知表现的影响没有明确的生理驱动因素。

sentences.en: Such studies are supported by assessment of CO2-induced changes in human brainwaves, measured by electroencephalography (EEG) combined with cognitive tests (reviewed in (Zhang et al., 2024)).
sentences.zh: 此类研究得到了对 CO2 引起的人脑电波变化的评估的支持，这些变化通过脑电图 (EEG) 结合认知测试进行测量（Zhang 等人，2024 年进行了综述）。

sentences.en: Such studies show that exposure to CO2 between 1,000 and 2,500 ppm results in heightened brain activity.
sentences.zh: 此类研究表明，接触 1,000 至 2,500 ppm 的二氧化碳会导致大脑活动增强。

sentences.en: Although the mechanisms underlying these impacts are not clear, Stumm (2023) postulates that CO2 induced increase in extracellular calcium ions may restrict the movement of sodium ions thereby reducing neuron excitability (Stumm, 2023).
sentences.zh: 尽管这些影响背后的机制尚不清楚，但 Stumm (2023) 假设 CO2 诱导的细胞外钙离子增加可能会限制钠离子的运动，从而降低神经元的兴奋性 (Stumm, 2023)。

sentences.en: Another explanation may be that CO2 signalling activates the autonomic nervous system causing stress which affects cognitive performance (Azuma et al., 2018).
sentences.zh: 另一种解释可能是二氧化碳信号传导会激活自主神经系统，从而产生压力，从而影响认知表现（Azuma et al., 2018）。

sentences.en: Brain activity holds paramount importance for human functioning, acting as the central regulator that coordinates various bodily functions and cognitive processes (Zhang et al., 2024).
sentences.zh: 大脑活动对于人类功能至关重要，充当协调各种身体功能和认知过程的中央调节器（Zhang et al., 2024）。

sentences.en: Given that CO2 exposure at moderate levels appears to prove detrimental to brain activity, additional research is urgently required to assess whether life-time exposure to CO2 levels predicted by climate change modelling will have significant impacts on human cognitive function.
sentences.zh: 鉴于事实证明，中等水平的二氧化碳暴露似乎对大脑活动有害，因此迫切需要进行更多研究来评估气候变化模型预测的终生暴露于二氧化碳水平是否会对人类认知功能产生重大影响。

blocks.html_en: Carbon dioxide is also known to cause anxiety and panic attacks in humans (Battaglia, 2017). CO2 sensitivity is one of the most basic and general alarm/avoidance systems within the realm of biology. While panic and anxiety attacks generally occur at high levels of CO2, the distribution of liability to CO2 sensitivity is continuous and normally distributed in humans and animals. This means that there are potentially small anxiety increases even at the current and near-future elevated levels of atmospheric CO2. To this effect, increased hormones associated with anxiety have been observed in mammals at levels of CO2 in the range 700–1000 ppm (Kiray et al., 2014; Martrette et al., 2017; Wyrwoll et al., 2022). Even a small permanent increase in global human anxiety could have a dangerous impact on societies being associated with greater fear, mental disturbance, conflicts, etc.

sentences.en: Carbon dioxide is also known to cause anxiety and panic attacks in humans (Battaglia, 2017).
sentences.zh: 众所周知，二氧化碳还会引起人类焦虑和恐慌（Battaglia，2017）。

sentences.en: CO2 sensitivity is one of the most basic and general alarm/avoidance systems within the realm of biology.
sentences.zh: CO2 敏感性是生物学领域中最基本和最通用的警报/避免系统之一。

sentences.en: While panic and anxiety attacks generally occur at high levels of CO2, the distribution of liability to CO2 sensitivity is continuous and normally distributed in humans and animals.
sentences.zh: 虽然惊恐和焦虑发作通常发生在二氧化碳浓度较高的情况下，但对二氧化碳敏感性的分布是连续的，并且在人类和动物中呈正态分布。

sentences.en: This means that there are potentially small anxiety increases even at the current and near-future elevated levels of atmospheric CO2.
sentences.zh: 这意味着，即使在当前和不久的将来大气二氧化碳浓度升高的情况下，焦虑也可能会略有增加。

sentences.en: To this effect, increased hormones associated with anxiety have been observed in mammals at levels of CO2 in the range 700–1000 ppm (Kiray et al., 2014; Martrette et al., 2017; Wyrwoll et al., 2022).
sentences.zh: 为此，在二氧化碳浓度为 700-1000 ppm 的哺乳动物中，观察到与焦虑相关的激素增加（Kiray 等人，2014 年；Martrette 等人，2017 年；Wyrwoll 等人，2022 年）。

sentences.en: Even a small permanent increase in global human anxiety could have a dangerous impact on societies being associated with greater fear, mental disturbance, conflicts, etc.
sentences.zh: 即使全球人类焦虑的小幅永久性增加也可能对与更大的恐惧、精神障碍、冲突等相关的社会产生危险的影响。

blocks.html_en: Health effects of long-term decreases in calcium and phosphorus

sentences.en: Health effects of long-term decreases in calcium and phosphorus
sentences.zh: 长期减少钙和磷对健康的影响

blocks.html_en: Both calcium and phosphorus are essential for human health, with insufficient levels (hypocalcemia and hypophosphatemia) being associated with a range of adverse health impacts (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005; Pepe et al., 2020).

sentences.en: Both calcium and phosphorus are essential for human health, with insufficient levels (hypocalcemia and hypophosphatemia) being associated with a range of adverse health impacts (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005; Pepe et al., 2020).
sentences.zh: 钙和磷对人类健康至关重要，含量不足（低钙血症和低磷血症）会导致一系列不利的健康影响（Gaasbeek & Meinders，2005；Pepe 等，2020）。

blocks.html_en: Calcium is essential to maintaining total body health, with low levels potentially impacting virtually any organ or system (Pepe et al., 2020). For example, calcium is essential in muscle contraction, oocyte activation, building strong bones and teeth, blood clotting, nerve impulse, transmission, regulating heart-beat and fluid balance within cells (Pravina et al., 2013). The requirements are greatest during periods of growth such as childhood. Low blood calcium (hypocalcemia) produces symptoms including muscle cramps, lethargy, numbness and tingling in the fingers, and problems with heart rhythm. Systemic Ca2+ is regarded as a hormone itself, as it can modulate the function of the parathyroid gland, the thyroid gland, the kidney, and other organs and cells via the calcium-sensing receptor (Proudfoot, 2019).

sentences.en: Calcium is essential to maintaining total body health, with low levels potentially impacting virtually any organ or system (Pepe et al., 2020).
sentences.zh: 钙对于维持全身健康至关重要，钙含量低可能会影响几乎任何器官或系统（Pepe 等人，2020）。

sentences.en: For example, calcium is essential in muscle contraction, oocyte activation, building strong bones and teeth, blood clotting, nerve impulse, transmission, regulating heart-beat and fluid balance within cells (Pravina et al., 2013).
sentences.zh: 例如，钙对于肌肉收缩、卵母细胞激活、强健骨骼和牙齿、血液凝固、神经冲动、传输、调节心跳和细胞内液体平衡至关重要（Pravina 等，2013）。

sentences.en: The requirements are greatest during periods of growth such as childhood.
sentences.zh: 在成长时期（例如童年），这种要求是最高的。

sentences.en: Low blood calcium (hypocalcemia) produces symptoms including muscle cramps, lethargy, numbness and tingling in the fingers, and problems with heart rhythm.
sentences.zh: 低血钙（低钙血症）会产生包括肌肉痉挛、嗜睡、手指麻木和刺痛以及心律问题等症状。

sentences.en: Systemic Ca2+ is regarded as a hormone itself, as it can modulate the function of the parathyroid gland, the thyroid gland, the kidney, and other organs and cells via the calcium-sensing receptor (Proudfoot, 2019).
sentences.zh: 全身Ca2+本身被视为一种激素，因为它可以通过钙敏感受体调节甲状旁腺、甲状腺、肾脏以及其他器官和细胞的功能（Proudfoot，2019）。

blocks.html_en: Similarly, phosphorus plays many critical roles in the body (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005). These include metabolic processes within the cell such as energy metabolism and protein phosphorylation which are key mechanisms that control many cellular functions, including metabolism, growth, and muscle contraction (Shaker & Deftos, 2023). It is a vital component of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), the body’s primary energy carrying molecule (Bonora et al., 2012), and 2,3-diphosphoglycerate (2,3-DPG), which is found in erythrocytes and is crucial in oxygen transport (Macdonald, 1977). Accordingly, abnormally low levels of phosphate can lead to tissue hypoxia and cellular function disruption (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005). Phosphorus also plays a role in nucleotide metabolism which is used to build DNA, RNA and it influences phospholipid metabolism that forms a vital part of cell growth and function (Shaker & Deftos, 2023).

sentences.en: Similarly, phosphorus plays many critical roles in the body (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005).
sentences.zh: 同样，磷在体内发挥着许多关键作用（Gaasbeek & Meinders，2005）。

sentences.en: These include metabolic processes within the cell such as energy metabolism and protein phosphorylation which are key mechanisms that control many cellular functions, including metabolism, growth, and muscle contraction (Shaker & Deftos, 2023).
sentences.zh: 其中包括细胞内的代谢过程，例如能量代谢和蛋白质磷酸化，它们是控制许多细胞功能的关键机制，包括新陈代谢、生长和肌肉收缩 (Shaker & Deftos, 2023)。

sentences.en: It is a vital component of adenosine triphosphate (ATP), the body’s primary energy carrying molecule (Bonora et al., 2012), and 2,3-diphosphoglycerate (2,3-DPG), which is found in erythrocytes and is crucial in oxygen transport (Macdonald, 1977).
sentences.zh: 它是人体主要能量携带分子三磷酸腺苷 (ATP) 和 2,3-二磷酸甘油酸 (2,3-DPG) 的重要组成部分，而 2,3-二磷酸甘油酸存在于红细胞中，对于氧气运输至关重要（Macdonald, 1977）。

sentences.en: Accordingly, abnormally low levels of phosphate can lead to tissue hypoxia and cellular function disruption (Gaasbeek & Meinders, 2005).
sentences.zh: 因此，异常低水平的磷酸盐会导致组织缺氧和细胞功能破坏（Gaasbeek & Meinders，2005）。

sentences.en: Phosphorus also plays a role in nucleotide metabolism which is used to build DNA, RNA and it influences phospholipid metabolism that forms a vital part of cell growth and function (Shaker & Deftos, 2023).
sentences.zh: 磷还在用于构建 DNA、RNA 的核苷酸代谢中发挥作用，并影响构成细胞生长和功能重要组成部分的磷脂代谢（Shaker & Deftos，2023）。

blocks.html_en: Conclusions

sentences.en: Conclusions
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: The analysis of average blood HCO3− levels in a large human population shows an increase between the years 2000 and 2020, while both calcium and phosphorus show a decrease over the same period. The increase in average blood HCO3− (~ 0.34% per year) is comparable to the increase in atmospheric CO2 levels (~ 0.5% per year). This trend suggests there may be a causal link between ambient CO2 and systemic bicarbonate levels, a finding that warrants additional attention. Trend analysis suggests that HCO3− levels will be at the currently accepted limit of the healthy range within 50 years, with calcium and phosphorus reaching the currently accepted minimum levels shortly after. Clearly these are estimations, as there are uncertainties due to factors such as variations in the numbers of participants, their environment leading up to blood sampling and differences in measurement procedures during each collection. Also, the relationships may not be linear. However, on the face of it, this raw analysis of biochemical data suggests the distinct possibility that, within a half century from now, HCO3− levels in human blood will reach unhealthy levels. What effects this may have on physiology remain to be elucidated, but urgently need to be considered.

sentences.en: The analysis of average blood HCO3− levels in a large human population shows an increase between the years 2000 and 2020, while both calcium and phosphorus show a decrease over the same period.
sentences.zh: 对大量人群的平均血液 HCO3− 水平的分析显示，2000 年至 2020 年间，该水平有所上升，而同期钙和磷的水平均有所下降。

sentences.en: The increase in average blood HCO3− (~ 0.34% per year) is comparable to the increase in atmospheric CO2 levels (~ 0.5% per year).
sentences.zh: 平均血液 HCO3− 的增加（每年约 0.34%）与大气 CO2 水平的增加（每年约 0.5%）相当。

sentences.en: This trend suggests there may be a causal link between ambient CO2 and systemic bicarbonate levels, a finding that warrants additional attention.
sentences.zh: 这一趋势表明环境二氧化碳和全身碳酸氢盐水平之间可能存在因果关系，这一发现值得额外关注。

sentences.en: Trend analysis suggests that HCO3− levels will be at the currently accepted limit of the healthy range within 50 years, with calcium and phosphorus reaching the currently accepted minimum levels shortly after.
sentences.zh: 趋势分析表明，HCO3− 水平将在 50 年内达到目前可接受的健康范围极限，钙和磷不久后将达到目前可接受的最低水平。

sentences.en: Clearly these are estimations, as there are uncertainties due to factors such as variations in the numbers of participants, their environment leading up to blood sampling and differences in measurement procedures during each collection.
sentences.zh: 显然，这些只是估计，因为由于参与者数量的变化、血液采样时的环境以及每次采集期间测量程序的差异等因素，存在不确定性。

sentences.en: Also, the relationships may not be linear.
sentences.zh: 此外，这些关系可能不是线性的。

sentences.en: However, on the face of it, this raw analysis of biochemical data suggests the distinct possibility that, within a half century from now, HCO3− levels in human blood will reach unhealthy levels.
sentences.zh: 然而，从表面上看，对生化数据的原始分析表明，从现在起的半个世纪内，人类血液中的 HCO3− 水平将达到不健康水平的明显可能性。

sentences.en: What effects this may have on physiology remain to be elucidated, but urgently need to be considered.
sentences.zh: 这可能对生理学产生什么影响仍有待阐明，但迫切需要考虑。

blocks.html_en: Given that the entire evolution period for humans has seen a stable and relatively low atmospheric CO2 level (< 300 ppm), it’s possible that our physiology is finely tuned for a range of CO2 that will not be much greater than this level. As the atmospheric CO2 levels rise, already at 420 pm, the increasing levels of bicarbonate, and decreasing levels of calcium and phosphorus in our blood represent permanent and growing changes in human blood chemistry. These changes can be explained by CO2 retention and overload in the body. The extra CO2 being inhaled isn’t expelled via increased ventilation, as ventilation rate is controlled by pH which has likely remained stable due to acid-base regulation. CO2 storage in the body then becomes a major issue presenting a risk to population health and an existential threat for many species especially given that the rise in atmospheric CO2 may be a phenomenon that has significant momentum. Realistically, to mitigate this approaching threat, the alarm needs to be raised immediately. Unfortunately, currently there is little awareness or inclination for action on this issue.

sentences.en: Given that the entire evolution period for humans has seen a stable and relatively low atmospheric CO2 level (< 300 ppm), it’s possible that our physiology is finely tuned for a range of CO2 that will not be much greater than this level.
sentences.zh: 鉴于人类的整个进化时期都经历了稳定且相对较低的大气二氧化碳水平（< 300 ppm），我们的生理机能可能针对不会比这个水平高出很多的二氧化碳范围进行了微调。

sentences.en: As the atmospheric CO2 levels rise, already at 420 pm, the increasing levels of bicarbonate, and decreasing levels of calcium and phosphorus in our blood represent permanent and growing changes in human blood chemistry.
sentences.zh: 随着大气中二氧化碳浓度的上升（下午 420 点），我们血液中碳酸氢盐浓度的增加以及钙和磷浓度的下降代表着人体血液化学成分发生永久性且不断增长的变化。

sentences.en: These changes can be explained by CO2 retention and overload in the body.
sentences.zh: 这些变化可以用体内二氧化碳滞留和超负荷来解释。

sentences.en: The extra CO2 being inhaled isn’t expelled via increased ventilation, as ventilation rate is controlled by pH which has likely remained stable due to acid-base regulation.
sentences.zh: 吸入的额外二氧化碳不会通过增加通气量排出，因为通气率由 pH 值控制，而 pH 值可能由于酸碱调节而保持稳定。

sentences.en: CO2 storage in the body then becomes a major issue presenting a risk to population health and an existential threat for many species especially given that the rise in atmospheric CO2 may be a phenomenon that has significant momentum.
sentences.zh: 体内的二氧化碳储存成为一个主要问题，对人口健康构成威胁，并对许多物种的生存构成威胁，特别是考虑到大气中二氧化碳的上升可能是一种势头强劲的现象。

sentences.en: Realistically, to mitigate this approaching threat, the alarm needs to be raised immediately.
sentences.zh: 实际上，为了减轻这种迫近的威胁，需要立即发出警报。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, currently there is little awareness or inclination for action on this issue.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，目前人们对此问题的认识或行动很少。

blocks.html_en: Larcombe, A.N., Bierwirth, P.N. Carbon dioxide overload, detected in human blood, suggests a potentially toxic atmosphere within 50 years. Air Qual Atmos Health 19, 44 (2026). https://doi.org/10.1007/s11869-026-01918-5

sentences.en: Larcombe, A.N., Bierwirth, P.N.
sentences.zh: 拉科姆 (Larcombe)，A.N.，比尔沃斯 (Bierwirth)，P.N.

sentences.en: Carbon dioxide overload, detected in human blood, suggests a potentially toxic atmosphere within 50 years.
sentences.zh: 在人体血液中检测到的二氧化碳超载表明，50 年内可能存在有毒大气。
